command.text: find /testbed/dir1/subdir1 -type f -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | awk '{print $1}' | sort | md5sum

raw_output_payload.content: find /testbed/dir1/subdir1 -type f -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | awk '{print $1}' | sort | md5sum
raw_output_payload.reasoning:
We need to find all .py files under /testbed/dir1/subdir1, compute md5 of each file, sort the list of md5 sums, then compute md5 of that sorted list. Likely we need to output a bash command that does this. So something like:

find /testbed/dir1/subdir1 -type f -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | awk '{print $1}' | sort | tr '\n' ' ' | md5sum

But need to output the md5 of the sorted list of md5 sums. The sorted list could be newline separated. So maybe:

find ... | sort | xargs -d '\n' md5sum? Wait we need md5 of each file, then sort those md5s. So:

find /testbed/dir1/subdir1 -type f -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | awk '{print $1}' | sort | tr '\n' ' ' | md5sum

But better to preserve newlines: use printf "%s\n" and then md5sum. So:

find /testbed/dir1/subdir1 -type f -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | awk '{print $1}' | sort | tr '\n' '\0' | md5sum

But md5sum expects input. We can pipe sorted md5s into md5sum. So:

find /testbed/dir1/subdir1 -type f -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | awk '{print $1}' | sort | tr '\n' '\0' | md5sum

But tr '\n' '\0' will produce null separated, not good. Instead just keep newlines: sort | md5sum. So:

find /testbed/dir1/subdir1 -type f -name '*.py' -print0 | xargs -0 md5sum | awk '{print $1}' | sort | md5sum

That outputs md5 of the sorted list of md5s. So final command.

task.query: Calculate the md5 sum of the sorted list of md5 sums of all ".py" files under /testbed/dir1/subdir1
task.gold: find /testbed/dir1/subdir1 -type f -name *.py -exec md5sum {} + | awk '{print $1}' | sort | md5sum
